essence: Collects and analyses soil samples to assess contamination, fertility, and environmental impact.

identity.title: Soil Technician
identity.summary: Performs field sampling and laboratory testing on soil to detect contaminants, assess quality, and support environmental, agricultural, and construction projects.

salary.notes: Technicians working in contaminated‑land remediation, geotechnical labs, or environmental consultancies can exceed £48k-£60k+.

qualifications: Training in environmental science, soil science, or laboratory testing
Knowledge of sampling techniques and contamination risks
Ability to operate laboratory instruments for soil analysis
Understanding of environmental regulations and reporting standards

lifestyle.description: Soil technicians work for environmental consultancies, laboratories, agricultural services, and construction firms. They collect samples from sites, prepare them for testing, analyse results, and support environmental assessments. The role suits individuals who enjoy hands‑on science, fieldwork, and contributing to environmental protection and land‑quality management.

habits: Collects soil samples from field sites
Prepares and analyses samples in the lab
